# T1617 — Hooking

**Tactics:** Defense Evasion  ·  **Platforms:** Android

## Description

Adversaries may utilize hooking to hide the presence of artifacts associated with their behaviors to evade detection. Hooking can be used to modify return values or data structures of system APIs and function calls. This process typically involves using 3rd party root frameworks, such as Xposed or Magisk, with either a system exploit or pre-existing root access. By including custom modules for root frameworks, adversaries can hook system APIs and alter the return value and/or system data structures to alter functionality/visibility of various aspects of the system.

## Detection

**Detection of Hooking**
- Correlates (1) device posture changes indicating root or elevated privilege state, (2) runtime framework manipulation or injection into application processes, and (3) anomalous API behavior or suppressed security signals. The defender observes a causal chain where an application gains privileged execution context, interacts with system frameworks (e.g., ART/Zygote), and modifies expected API outputs or suppresses security-relevant signals such as permission checks, sensor access reporting, or process visibility. _(log sources: MobileEDR:telemetry, android:MDMLog)_

## Mitigations

- **Attestation** (M1002): Enable remote attestation capabilities when available (such as Android SafetyNet or Samsung Knox TIMA Attestation) and prohibit devices that fail the attestation from accessing enterprise resources.
- **Deploy Compromised Device Detection Method** (M1010): A variety of methods exist that can be used to enable enterprises to identify compromised (e.g. rooted/jailbroken) devices, whether using security mechanisms built directly into the device, third-party mobile security applications, enterprise mobility management (EMM)/mobile device management (MDM) capabilities, or other methods. Some methods may be trivial to evade while others may be more sophisticated.

## Reference

- ATT&CK: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1617
